headline: CONTANGO TARGETS 40,000 METRES IN $63M DRILL PUSH ACROSS ALASKA AND BC'S GOLDEN TRIANGLE
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold — formed from the March 2026 merger of Contango ORE and Dolly Varden Silver — outlined a $63 million 2026 exploration program anchored by a $25 million, 40,000-metre infill drill campaign at Kitsault Valley in BC's Golden Triangle plus $21 million at its Lucky Shot gold project in Alaska. A further $17 million is allocated to infrastructure work at Johnson Tract.

headline: THIRD PORPHYRY IN TWO YEARS: Arras Minerals hits 115.7m at 1.44 g/t Au and 0.24% Cu in Kazakhstan
snippet: Arras Minerals (TSXV: ARK) confirmed a third porphyry centre on the Berezski Trend in northeastern Kazakhstan, intersecting 115.7 metres at 1.44 g/t gold and 0.24% copper at Berezski East — its third distinct porphyry discovery on the property in two years. The back-to-back success prompted the company to expand its Phase II drill program from 20,000 to 30,000 metres.

headline: Ur-Energy starts uranium mining at second Wyoming ISR mine, targeting 2M lbs U₃O₈ annually
snippet: Ur-Energy (TSX: URE / NYSE: URG) announced the start of mining operations at its Shirley Basin in-situ recovery project in Wyoming — the company's second producing uranium mine — with uranium-bearing solution now flowing from Mine Unit 1. Processed resin is expected to ship to the Lost Creek processing facility this summer; the project is licensed for up to 2 million pounds U₃O₈ per year with an approximately nine-year mine life.

headline: Vizsla Silver awards $170M EPCM contract for Panuco silver-gold project, advancing Mexico mine toward construction
snippet: Vizsla Silver awarded a $170 million EPCM contract to M3 Engineering and a $50 million mine design contract to Mining Plus for its Panuco silver-gold project in Mexico. The agreements are key milestones following the November 2025 feasibility study and move Panuco into active pre-construction engineering.

headline: Gold slides to $4,736 as Strait of Hormuz blockage keeps inflation risk elevated; up $1,448 year-over-year
snippet: Gold retreated to $4,736 per ounce on April 23 as the ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockage kept energy prices high and inflation risks elevated, partially offsetting early US-Iran diplomatic signals. The metal remains up $1,448 versus a year ago, with Goldman Sachs and Bank of America both maintaining $6,000 year-end targets.

headline: Serra Verde maps output surge for all four magnetic rare earths ahead of $2.8B USA Rare Earth acquisition close
snippet: Ahead of the $2.8 billion acquisition by USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR), Brazil's Serra Verde outlined plans to significantly expand production at its Pela Ema mine — the only non-Asian facility capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths (Nd, Pr, Dy, Tb) at scale. A 15-year US government offtake agreement covering 100% of production underpins the deal, which is expected to close in Q3 2026.

headline: SIXTH STRAIGHT YEAR: Silver deficit widens to 46.3M oz in 2026 as 762M oz drawn from stockpiles since 2021
snippet: The Silver Institute's World Silver Survey 2026 projects a 46.3 million troy ounce deficit for the year — up 15% from 2025 and the sixth consecutive annual supply shortfall. A cumulative 762 million ounces have been drawn from above-ground stockpiles since 2021, a drawdown analysts describe as having no modern precedent.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 790+ TONNES SILVER in early 2026 — solar demand keeping structural deficit intact, Gold SWOT finds
snippet: The latest Gold SWOT from US Global Investors highlights China's 790-plus tonne silver import figure for early 2026 — driven in part by demand for silver paste used in solar panel production — as evidence that underlying silver demand remains structurally robust. Silver has surged more than 150% over the past year despite retreating from its January 2026 all-time high of $121.62 per ounce.

headline: Goldman holds $12,000/t copper year-end target as Traxys projects $15,000 on deepening mine supply crunch
snippet: Goldman Sachs maintained its $12,000 per tonne copper year-end forecast while Traxys projected a surge to $15,000, both citing a 330,000-tonne refined supply deficit in 2026 driven by disruptions at Indonesia's Grasberg mine and AI/energy-transition demand. The wide range of forecasts reflects a market split between a genuine long-term supply shortage and near-term headwinds from LME inventory builds at six-year highs.

headline: Precious metals consolidate as US-Iran Strait of Hormuz standoff persists — gold, silver, PGMs all mark time
snippet: Gold, silver and platinum-group metals are trading sideways as markets weigh the high-stakes US-Iran Strait of Hormuz standoff against the possibility of diplomatic de-escalation. Analysts characterize the pause as healthy consolidation within an ongoing precious metals bull trend rather than a structural reversal.
